name: Week 2 KB Full Body
description: Same structure as W1 — squat, hinge, push, pull, core. Two swaps: high pulls replace swings in the hinge block (progression — more explosive pull), Turkish get-ups replace windmills in the core block (more complex movement, full-body coordination). Single bell, ~40 min.

exercises.notes: Light, loosen shoulders and thoracic spine
Same as W1 — aim for cleaner depth and more control. Full depth, elbows inside knees.
Hold KB goblet style. Rear foot on bench/chair. You know the movement now — push the depth.
Same as W1 — slow descent, squeeze glutes at top.
NEW — replaces swings. Explosive hip snap, pull the bell to shoulder height. More upper back work than swings.
Same as W1 — clean to rack, strict press. Try to go heavier or cleaner.
Same as W1 — hinge position, pull elbow past ribs.
NEW — replaces windmill. Go LIGHT. This is a complex full-body movement — learn the pattern. Look up the steps if needed.
Last push — full power, empty the tank. Same as W1.

tips: Same structure as W1 — you know the flow now, so it should feel smoother
High pulls are a progression from swings — same hip snap, but you pull the bell higher. More explosive.
Turkish get-ups: go VERY light the first time. This is skill work, not strength work yet.
If TGUs feel too awkward, do windmills instead — no shame, they're still great
Progress by adding reps, then adding a round, then going heavier
